reason: Check agent verifies cargo check / cargo test pass, the 3 commits are atomic, no dead code (e.g. ctx.cwd leftover if fully replaced), all 7 tools run in worktree (regression: shell pwd test).

Check agent verifies the boundary check is still correct: any tool path argument that escapes worktree_path is rejected. boundary::assert_within_root semantics preserved across the ctx.cwd → ctx.worktree_path switch.

Check agent verifies the new column is properly NULL-handled: (a) pre-step-4 sessions have NULL and tools fallback to current_cwd, (b) the ALTER migration is idempotent, (c) sessions created post-step-4 always have worktree_path set OR fail (if is_git_repo=false).

Check agent verifies worktree-related errors propagate cleanly to UI: (a) non-git project create error message is clear, (b) worktree cleanup error on session delete is best-effort (logged not blocking).
Check agent verifies DiffView follows component guidelines: scoped CSS, <script setup>, props typed, XSS-safe (jsdiff output is plain text, no v-html).
Check agent verifies vue-tsc passes and the new IPC types match the Rust struct (no type drift between Rust serde and TS interface).

Check agent verifies the diff payload types are correct: per-file diff is plain text (string), file list is array of {path: string, added: number, removed: number, status: 'added'|'modified'|'deleted'}. No `any`.
Check agent verifies the git2-rs usage matches research: commit/diff/worktree_add are git2-rs calls; worktree remove is the ONE spawn fallback. No accidental reimplementation of available API.
Check agent verifies DiffView uses jsdiff, not Monaco or vue-diff-view. Bundle size sanity check (Monaco would balloon to MB scale).
Check agent cross-references the 8 R's + 8 AC's to ensure each PR covers its scope. No scope creep (no auto-commit, no LRU, no merge).
Check agent verifies the IPC contract (Rust struct → TS type → Pinia store → component prop) is consistent end-to-end. The diff payload struct definition appears in 3+ places; they must agree.
Check agent verifies ToolContext change is in 1 place, not 7 — all 7 tools consume ctx.worktree_path through the same field, not via 7 separate hardcoded fallbacks.
